message:
fix(core,pager): add is_readonly to MvccPager trait, harden WAL recovery probing, validate WAL page size

Pager trait (traits.rs):
- Added is_readonly() method to MvccPager trait, returning whether the
  pager was opened in read-only mode. This is needed by the connection
  layer to decide whether WAL checkpoint operations are safe during
  close. Implemented for SimplePager (delegates to inner.access_mode),
  MockMvccPager (returns false), and MemoryMockMvccPager (returns false).

Core connection (connection.rs):
- Added is_readonly() to PagerBackend that dispatches across all VFS
  backends (Memory, IoUring, Unix, Windows)
- Reverted the READWRITE→READONLY fallback in install_wal_backend_with_vfs
  that was added in the previous commit. WAL installation for write-path
  transactions must use READWRITE opens; silently falling back to READONLY
  could produce a WAL backend that appears functional but fails on the
  first commit with a confusing error. The existing install_existing_wal_backend
  (read-only path) already handles READONLY opens correctly.
- Changed test_open_live_c_sqlite_wal_database... to use explicit
  conn.close() instead of drop(conn), with an assertion that close
  succeeds without attempting to checkpoint through a read-only WAL handle
- Added ReadWriteWalInstallProbeVfs test helper and
  test_install_wal_backend_does_not_fallback_to_read_only_open test
  that verifies the write-path WAL installer fails cleanly when READWRITE
  open fails, rather than silently retrying READONLY

Pager WAL recovery (pager.rs):
- Strengthened stale_main_header_can_be_recovered_from_live_wal to
  actually parse the WAL file header and validate that the WAL's
  page size matches the page size extracted from the stale main-file
  header bytes. Previously it only checked WAL file size >= header size,
  which could accept corrupt or mismatched WAL files. Now uses
  WalFile::open to parse the header and compares page sizes.
- Reformatted the VFS open call for the WAL probe to be more readable

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
